failedAssertions.reason: Security enforcement is incomplete for repo installs: zip upload protections exist, but repo archive extraction uses raw extractall without traversal guard in install_from_repo, leaving an explicit validation gap.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated backend testing surface to prefer localhost:8000 command API and openapi health check; documented 8899 as legacy surface.
Aligned backend service port/healthcheck to localhost:8000 with /openapi.json for reliable setup checks.

frictions: Assigned account/password were not needed because this surface did not require an auth/login interaction for the tested flows.
Backend /health on port 8000 returned 404 while /command endpoints were healthy.
Backend health endpoint at /health on port 8000 returned 404 while /command and /channel endpoints were healthy.
Backend health probe at /health returned 404 while API root/openapi/command routes were healthy; used /openapi.json and command endpoints for validation.
Direct /channel/stream_flow_run call after uninstall returned RunStarted/RunCompleted without surfacing the unknown-node error in this setup.
Direct channel payload must send {body:{...}} for stream_flow_run; unwrapped payload returns missing-body error.
In-session chrome-devtools browser automation was blocked by an existing locked profile.
Live install from arbitrary GitHub sample repo URL returned 404, so VAL-EXT-001 relied on targeted unit tests for successful repo-install path confirmation.
Provided credentials were not usable because this web surface did not present a login step during assigned flows.
agent-browser returned a transient 'Resource temporarily unavailable (os error 35)' once while waiting for navigation.
blockersObserved: Could not start new chrome-devtools-controlled browser session because shared profile was already in use.
